question: Why is Ada Lovelace important?
answer:
Ada Lovelace is important because she is widely credited with writing the first algorithm intended for a machine and with describing the Analytical Engine in a way that anticipated general-purpose computing. [1][2]

Sources:
[1] Ada Lovelace — https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ada_Lovelace
    Ada Lovelace was an English mathematician and writer, chiefly known for her work on Charles Babbage's proposed mechanical general-purpose computer, the Analytical Engine.
[2] Analytical engine — https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Analytical_engine
    The Analytical Engine was a proposed mechanical general-purpose computer designed by Charles Babbage.

question: What did the Analytical Engine do?
answer:
The Analytical Engine was designed as a mechanical general-purpose computer that could perform calculations under program control using punched cards. [1]

Sources:
[1] Analytical engine — https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Analytical_engine
    The Analytical Engine was a proposed mechanical general-purpose computer designed by Charles Babbage.

sources.title: Analytical engine
sources.excerpt: The Analytical Engine was a proposed mechanical general-purpose computer designed by Charles Babbage.

question: How did Charles Babbage influence early computing?
answer:
Charles Babbage influenced early computing by designing the Difference Engine and the Analytical Engine, which introduced ideas about programmable mechanical computation decades before modern computers. [1][2]

Sources:
[1] Charles Babbage — https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Charles_Babbage
    Charles Babbage was an English mathematician, philosopher, inventor and mechanical engineer.
[2] Analytical engine — https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Analytical_engine
    The Analytical Engine was a proposed mechanical general-purpose computer designed by Charles Babbage.

question: What were the main causes of World War I?
answer:
World War I grew out of militarism, alliances, imperialism, and nationalism, with the assassination of Archduke Franz Ferdinand triggering the immediate crisis in 1914. [1]

Sources:
[1] World War I — https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/World_War_I
    The war grew from tensions in Europe over militarism, alliances, imperialism, and nationalism.

sources.title: World War I
sources.excerpt: The war grew from tensions in Europe over militarism, alliances, imperialism, and nationalism.

question: What was the significance of the Berlin Wall?
answer:
The Berlin Wall became a symbol of the Cold War by dividing East and West Berlin and representing the conflict between Soviet control and Western democracy until its fall in 1989. [1]

Sources:
[1] Berlin Wall — https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Berlin_Wall
    The Berlin Wall divided Berlin from 1961 to 1989 and symbolized the Iron Curtain.

sources.title: Berlin Wall
sources.excerpt: The Berlin Wall divided Berlin from 1961 to 1989 and symbolized the Iron Curtain.

question: How did the Meiji Restoration change Japan?
answer:
The Meiji Restoration transformed Japan by ending the shogunate, restoring imperial rule, and accelerating modernization through industrialization, military reform, and new institutions. [1]

Sources:
[1] Meiji Restoration — https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Meiji_Restoration
    The Meiji Restoration restored practical imperial rule to Japan in 1868 under Emperor Meiji.

sources.title: Meiji Restoration
sources.excerpt: The Meiji Restoration restored practical imperial rule to Japan in 1868 under Emperor Meiji.

question: What was the Silk Road?
answer:
The Silk Road was a network of trade routes connecting East Asia with the Mediterranean world, carrying goods, technologies, religions, and ideas across Eurasia. [1]

Sources:
[1] Silk Road — https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Silk_Road
    The Silk Road was a network of trade routes connecting the East and West.

sources.title: Silk Road
sources.excerpt: The Silk Road was a network of trade routes connecting the East and West.

question: How did the French Revolution begin?
answer:
The French Revolution began in 1789 amid financial crisis, social inequality, and political conflict, with the Estates-General and the storming of the Bastille becoming major early events. [1]

Sources:
[1] French Revolution — https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/French_Revolution
    The French Revolution began in 1789 and changed France's political order.

sources.title: French Revolution
sources.excerpt: The French Revolution began in 1789 and changed France's political order.

question: What did the Industrial Revolution change?
answer:
The Industrial Revolution shifted work from handcraft and agriculture to machine-powered factory manufacturing, reshaping cities, labor, and global trade. [1]

Sources:
[1] Industrial Revolution — https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Industrial_Revolution
    The Industrial Revolution was the transition to new manufacturing processes in Europe and the United States.

sources.title: Industrial Revolution
sources.excerpt: The Industrial Revolution was the transition to new manufacturing processes in Europe and the United States.

question: How did ancient Athens practice democracy?
answer:
Ancient Athens practiced direct democracy, where eligible citizens voted in the Assembly and served on councils and juries that helped make political decisions. [1]

Sources:
[1] Athenian democracy — https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Athenian_democracy
    Athenian democracy was a system of direct democracy developed in the 5th century BC.

sources.title: Athenian democracy
sources.excerpt: Athenian democracy was a system of direct democracy developed in the 5th century BC.

question: What caused the fall of the Western Roman Empire?
answer:
The Western Roman Empire fell because of political instability, military pressure, economic problems, and repeated invasions by external groups, culminating in the removal of the last emperor in 476 AD. [1]

Sources:
[1] Fall of the Western Roman Empire — https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fall_of_the_Western_Roman_Empire
    The Western Roman Empire fell in 476 AD after a long period of decline.

sources.title: Fall of the Western Roman Empire
sources.excerpt: The Western Roman Empire fell in 476 AD after a long period of decline.

question: Who was Martin Luther and why was he important?
answer:
Martin Luther was a German reformer whose criticism of indulgences and church authority helped spark the Protestant Reformation and reshape Western Christianity. [1]

Sources:
[1] Martin Luther — https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Martin_Luther
    Martin Luther was a German professor of theology, composer, priest, monk, and central figure in the Protestant Reformation.

question: What is the difference between BM25 and vector search?
answer:
BM25 ranks documents by keyword overlap and term frequency, while vector search matches semantic similarity using embeddings to find conceptually related passages. [1]

Sources:
[1] Information retrieval — https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Information_retrieval
    Traditional keyword search and semantic search are different retrieval methods.

sources.title: Information retrieval
sources.excerpt: Traditional keyword search and semantic search are different retrieval methods.

question: How should the model respond when evidence is weak?
answer: When evidence is weak, the model should say it cannot verify the claim from the retrieved sources instead of guessing or inventing a citation.
